title: 7 Reimbursement of Government Cheques and Dishonoured Payments
body_text:
7	Reimbursement of Government Cheques and Dishonoured Payments
7.1	Requesting Reimbursement for Fraudulent or Non-Fraudulent BC Employment and Assistance Cheques
The BC government issues BC Employment and Assistance Cheques as a form of income assistance. BC Employment and Assistance Imprest Cheques are issued to replace lost or stolen cheques or to replace rejected direct deposit payments. See the Deposit Processing Part for information on negotiating and verifying BC Employment and Assistance Cheques.
Fraudulent BC Employment and Assistance Cheques are defined in the BC Benefits Cheques Indemnity Agreement as those that are counterfeit, bear unauthorized material alterations to the face amounts or Payees, or have forged endorsements or forged Drawer signatures.
Non-fraudulent cheques are authentic BC Employment and Assistance cheques that were wrongfully cashed (accidental duplicate deposit). The process for requesting indemnity on a non-fraudulent item is the same as for fraudulent items. The BC government will reimburse for a duplicate payment on a case-by-case basis where
the item was deposited by a non-member and the financial institution met all ID requirements, and
the same item was deposited through remote deposit.
The BC government will indemnify financial institutions that participate in the BC Benefits Cheques Indemnity Agreement against fraud if encashment procedures and identification requirements are followed. This indemnification includes the following maximum face values:
$4,500 for regular monthly BC Employment and Assistance Cheques
$3,000 for Imprest cheques issued by the Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction
$3,000 for Imprest cheques issue by the Ministry of Children and Family Development
Note:	These are the current limits at time of publication.
Note:	Cheques returned for the reason 芒聙聹payment stopped芒聙聺 aren芒聙聶t classified as fraudulent cheques and consequently aren芒聙聶t specifically covered by the indemnity agreement. However, the province can honour reimbursement claims for these items if you state 芒聙聹holder in due course芒聙聺 as the reason for the claim. Process the claim in accordance with the indemnity agreement but note that there is no guarantee of reimbursement.
The following items must be included with the claim you submit for reimbursement:
The original cheque, a certified true copy, or a bank-issued Return Replacement Document (RRD) endorsed with the customer芒聙聶s signature.
A letter requesting reimbursement, including but not limited to
芒聢聮
Details of any funds that have already been recovered against the wrongful cashing of the Employment and Assistance cheque
芒聢聮
Details of the sequence of events and the diligence followed in the cashing of the Employment and Assistance cheque
芒聢聮
Confirmation that the cheque was endorsed by the individual believed to be the customer in front of an employee of your financial institution
Proof of two pieces of legitimate ID consistent with the Ministry芒聙聶s requirements taken to identify the customer at the time of cashing and referenced on the back of the cheque
Proof that the signature from the ID taken matches the signature endorsement on the back of the cheque
Proceed as follows to request reimbursement for a fraudulent or non-fraudulent BC Employment and Assistance Cheque:
Complete the Request for Reimbursement 芒聙聯 Fraudulent BC Employment and Assistance Cheque, Form 1294. Completion of the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Request for Reimbursement 芒聙聯 Fraudulent BC Employment and Assistance Cheque, Form 1294
Create a ServiceNow Paper Payments Trace Request ticket. See Chapter 8, Entering Manual Returns and Reimbursement Requests on ServiceNow, for procedures.
Attach the completed Request for Reimbursement 芒聙聯 Fraudulent BC Employment and Assistance Cheque, Form 1294, and other supporting documentation to the ServiceNow ticket.
Retain a copy of the form and any other documents associated with the reimbursement claim for your records.
Central 1 will credit reimbursement proceeds to your Canadian dollar current account once funds are received.
Reimbursement Disqualifications of BC Employment and Assistance Cheques
Financial institutions won芒聙聶t receive reimbursement for fraudulent cheques if
a reasonably prudent employee would have recognized that the cheque was fraudulent;
a reasonably prudent employee would have recognized that the identification provided by the person presenting the cheque wasn芒聙聶t genuine;
the encashment procedures provided in the BC Benefits Cheques Indemnity Agreement, Form 3366, weren芒聙聶t followed;
the face amount of the cheque was in excess of the maximum face amount;
the cheque was presented to a branch of an institution outside of BC;
the cheque wasn芒聙聶t endorsed in the presence of an employee or the Endorsement wasn芒聙聶t in the name of the Payee;
the aggregate face amount of fraudulent cheques encashed by the institution, where the indemnity applies, exceeds the maximum annual amount; or
the cheque was cashed by the institution as Drawee of the cheque, and to the credit of another financial institution.
Refer to the Deposit Processing Part for more information.
Related Information
Request for Reimbursement 芒聙聯 Fraudulent BC Employment and Assistance Cheque
,
Form 1294
BC Benefits Cheques Indemnity Agreement,
Form 3366
7.2	Requesting Reimbursement for Dishonoured Canada Revenue Agency or Receiver General Payments
Attempt to recover funds if your financial institution accepts a Canada Customs, GST/HST, excise, or federal taxation bill payment and the warrant used to make the payment is dishonoured by the Drawee financial institution.
If funds aren芒聙聶t recovered, proceed as follows to request reimbursement from the federal government:
Prepare a Request for Reimbursement 芒聙聯 Dishonoured Federal Government Payment
, Form 1299. Completion of the form is self-explanatory.
See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Request for Reimbursement 芒聙聯 Dishonoured Federal Government Payment, Form 1299
Create a ServiceNow Paper Payments Trace Request ticket. See Chapter 8, Entering Manual Returns and Reimbursement Requests on ServiceNow, for procedures.
Attach the completed Request for Reimbursement 芒聙聯 Dishonoured Federal Government Payment, Form 1299, an image of the Chargeback, and other supporting information to the ServiceNow ticket.
Retain a copy of the form
and other documents associated with the reimbursement claim
for your records.
Central 1 will credit your financial institution芒聙聶s Canadian dollar account once funds are received from the federal government.
Related Information
Request for Reimbursement 芒聙聯 Dishonoured Federal Government Payment
,
Form 1299
7.3	Requesting Reimbursement for Dishonoured Receiver General Warrants
Financial institutions must cash Receiver General Warrants up to a maximum of $1,750 for non-members. The Government of Canada indemnifies financial institutions up to $1,750 but only if they follow the identification and endorsement requirements of CPA Rule G8, Procedures/Criteria Pertaining to the Encashment of Receiver General Warrants for Non-customers. See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on negotiating Receiver General Warrants. If your financial institution cashed a warrant that exceeded $1,750, reimbursement will be paid up to a maximum of $1,750.
Note:	This is the current limit at time of publication.
CPA Rule G8 indemnifies financial institutions against counterfeit warrants, but not against material alterations. However, the Master Bond Program in BC may cover financial institutions for direct losses due to materially altered warrants. Financial institutions in Ontario and the Atlantic region can contact CUMIS for coverage of losses due to materially altered Receiver General Warrants.
When your financial institution receives a dishonoured Receiver General Warrant, you must try to recover funds before seeking reimbursement. Proceed as follows to request reimbursement from the federal government if funds can芒聙聶t be recovered within a reasonable time:
Complete the Request for Reimbursement 芒聙聯 Receiver General Warrant
, Form 2303
. Completion of the form is self-explanatory but note the following in Figure 3.
芒聭
If the warrant was cashed because of a personal reference (sponsorship), check the appropriate box on the form and ensure that it isn芒聙聶t signed by the same employee who initialled the back of the warrant.
Figure 3: Request for Reimbursement 芒聙聯 Receiver General Warrants, Form 2303
Create a ServiceNow Paper Payments Trace Request ticket. See Chapter 8, Entering Manual Returns and Reimbursement Requests on ServiceNow, for procedures.
Attach an image of the Chargeback, the affidavit that came with the returned item (if applicable), and the completed Request for Reimbursement 芒聙聯 Receiver General Warrants
, Form 2303,
to the ServiceNow ticket.
Retain a copy of the form and other documents associated with the reimbursement claim.
Central 1 will credit your financial institution芒聙聶s Canadian dollar account once funds are received from the federal government.
Related Information
Request for Reimbursement 芒聙聯 Receiver General Warrants
,
Form 2303
END
